tagged_story_for_tts:
"Gravitational sweet spots.<[silence:50ms]> Orbits that stay put.<[silence:50ms]>
The name honors Joseph-Louis Lagrange, who calculated where a small object could hover between two larger bodies without falling.<[silence:50ms]>
Earth and Sun create five such points: L1 to L5.<[silence:50ms]>
At L2, the James Webb Space Telescope orbits, shielded from solar heat, peering into deep space.<[silence:50ms]>
No fuel needed—just balance.<[silence:50ms]>
Three related subjects are orbital mechanics, celestial stability and space telescopes."
clean_story:
"Gravitational sweet spots. Orbits that stay put.
The name honors Joseph-Louis Lagrange, who calculated where a small object could hover between two larger bodies without falling.
Earth and Sun create five such points: L1 to L5.
At L2, the James Webb Space Telescope orbits, shielded from solar heat, peering into deep space.
No fuel needed—just balance.
Three related subjects are orbital mechanics, celestial stability and space telescopes."
